title: refactor: toApiError in socketServer duplicates AppError mapping already in errorHandler middleware
fix: attachmentController catches service errors by string-matching instead of AppError types

body:
## Description

\`friendController.ts\` is the only controller in the codebase that bypasses the service layer and calls the repository directly (\`makeFriendRepository\`). Every other controller (auth, user, room, message, folder, attachment) depends on a service that owns the business logic. This breaks the layered architecture and makes business rules harder to reuse, test, and maintain.

## Evidence

\`\`\`typescript
// backend/src/controllers/friendController.ts:14
export const makeFriendController = (
  repo: ReturnType<typeof makeFriendRepository>,  // ← repository, not a service
  notifyUser?: (userId: string, eventName: string, payload: any) => void
) => { ... }
\`\`\`

Business logic currently living in the controller:
- Self-request guard (\`userId === body.target_user_id\`)
- Block check (\`repo.isBlocked()\`) before sending a request

If another entry point (admin CLI, scheduled job, future GraphQL resolver) needs the same logic, it must duplicate these checks rather than calling a shared service.

## Tasks

- [ ] Create \`backend/src/services/friendService.ts\` with \`makeFriendService(repo, notifyUser?)\`
- [ ] Move business logic (self-request guard, block check) from the controller into the service
- [ ] Update \`friendController.ts\` to depend on the service, not the repository
- [ ] Update \`backend/src/index.ts\` composition root accordingly
- [ ] Update E2E and unit tests

## Acceptance criteria

- \`friendController\` depends on a \`FriendService\`, not \`makeFriendRepository\`
- All business rules (block check, self-request prevention) live in the service
- Existing friend-related E2E tests continue to pass
title: refactor: friendController calls repository directly, bypassing the service layer

body:
## Description

\`messageService.sendMessage()\` guards the \`resolveMentions\` call with a runtime \`'resolveMentions' in roomMemberRepo\` check and casts to \`any\`. However, \`resolveMentions\` is already a **required** method in \`IRoomMemberRepository\`, making the guard permanently redundant — and the \`as any\` cast permanently disables TypeScript's type-checking on that call site.

## Evidence

\`\`\`typescript
// backend/src/services/messageService.ts:53-57
if (mentionNames.length > 0) {
  if ('resolveMentions' in roomMemberRepo) {          // always true for conforming impl
    mentionedUserIds = await (roomMemberRepo as any).resolveMentions(  // escapes type system
      parsed.data.roomId, mentionNames
    );
  }
}
\`\`\`

\`\`\`typescript
// backend/src/repositories/IRoomMemberRepository.ts
resolveMentions(roomId: string, names: string[]): Promise<string[]>;  // required, not optional
\`\`\`

**Failure scenario:** The \`as any\` cast means any future signature change to \`resolveMentions\` (e.g. renamed parameter, changed return type) will not produce a compile error at this call site. If a non-conforming repo is ever injected, the guard evaluates to \`false\` and mentions are silently dropped with no error or log.

## Tasks

- [ ] Remove the \`'resolveMentions' in roomMemberRepo\` duck-type guard
- [ ] Remove the \`as any\` cast and call \`roomMemberRepo.resolveMentions()\` directly on the typed interface
- [ ] Ensure unit test mocks for \`IRoomMemberRepository\` include \`resolveMentions\`

## Acceptance criteria

- \`resolveMentions\` is called directly on the typed \`IRoomMemberRepository\` interface with no \`as any\`
- TypeScript compile error produced if \`resolveMentions\` signature changes and the call site is not updated
title: fix: dead duck-type guard in messageService bypasses IRoomMemberRepository type safety for resolveMentions

body:
## Description

\`addEmergencyContact\` in \`userController.ts\` calls \`service.getEmergencyContacts()\` before \`service.upsertEmergencyContact()\` solely to decide whether to return HTTP 201 (created) or 200 (updated). This introduces both a TOCTOU race and an unnecessary DB round-trip on every request.

## Evidence

\`\`\`typescript
// backend/src/controllers/userController.ts:57-62
const existing = await service.getEmergencyContacts(userId);          // DB query #1
const isUpdate = existing.some(c => c.contactId === parsed.data.contactId);
const contact = await service.upsertEmergencyContact(userId, parsed.data.contactId, parsed.data.message); // DB query #2
res.status(isUpdate ? 200 : 201).json(contact);
\`\`\`

The upsert SQL already executes \`ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE RETURNING *\` — the database already knows whether it inserted or updated, but that information is not surfaced to the caller.

**Race condition:** Two concurrent requests for the same \`(userId, contactId)\` both execute \`getEmergencyContacts\` before either reaches \`upsertEmergencyContact\` → both see \`isUpdate = false\` → both return 201, even though one of them performed an UPDATE.

## Tasks

- [ ] Surface insert-vs-update from the repository, e.g. via PostgreSQL \`xmax = 0\` trick in \`RETURNING\` or a CTE with an \`action\` flag
- [ ] Remove the pre-fetch \`getEmergencyContacts\` call from \`addEmergencyContact\`
- [ ] Add a test asserting concurrent upserts return the correct status codes

## Acceptance criteria

- \`addEmergencyContact\` issues a single DB operation (no pre-fetch SELECT)
- Returns 201 on first creation, 200 on subsequent updates, correctly under concurrent load
title: fix: addEmergencyContact has TOCTOU race and redundant DB query when determining 201 vs 200

body:
## Description

Cursor-based pagination in \`MessageRepository.findByRoom()\` uses \`sent_at\` alone as the cursor column. Because \`sent_at\` is not unique, two messages sharing an identical timestamp straddle a page boundary incorrectly: the message that is not the cursor is permanently excluded by the strict \`< cursor_timestamp\` condition.

## Evidence

\`\`\`sql
-- backend/src/repositories/messageRepository.ts:53-67
WHERE m.room_id = $1
  AND m.sent_at < (SELECT sent_at FROM messages WHERE message_id = $2 AND room_id = $1)
ORDER BY m.sent_at DESC LIMIT $3
-- ↑ Messages with sent_at = cursor_sent_at are always excluded, even if they aren't the cursor row
\`\`\`

**Failure scenario:** Messages X (\`sent_at=T\`) and Y (\`sent_at=T\`) exist in the same room. X is used as \`beforeId\` cursor → query returns only rows where \`sent_at < T\` → Y is silently skipped on every subsequent page with no gap indicator.

This is realistic: PostgreSQL \`DEFAULT now()\` has microsecond resolution, and concurrent inserts from multiple clients committing in the same microsecond are common in active chat rooms.

## Tasks

- [ ] Switch to a composite cursor: \`(sent_at DESC, message_id DESC)\`
- [ ] Update the \`WHERE\` clause to \`(m.sent_at, m.message_id) < (cursor_sent_at, cursor_message_id)\`
- [ ] Ensure a composite index on \`(room_id, sent_at DESC, message_id DESC)\` exists in migrations
- [ ] Update \`findByRoom\` signature to accept \`{ beforeId, limit }\` and fetch \`(sent_at, message_id)\` for the cursor internally

## Acceptance criteria

- Messages with identical \`sent_at\` are never skipped across page boundaries
- Pagination is deterministic regardless of concurrent message insertion order
title: fix: message pagination skips messages when multiple messages share the same sent_at timestamp

body:
## Description

The cursor-based pagination in \`MessageRepository.findByRoom()\` uses a subquery to fetch \`sent_at\` for the \`beforeId\` message, scoped to \`room_id = $1\`. When \`beforeId\` belongs to a different room the subquery returns no rows, propagating \`NULL\` into the outer \`WHERE m.sent_at < NULL\` clause — which is never true in SQL — causing the query to silently return zero rows.

## Evidence

\`\`\`sql
-- backend/src/repositories/messageRepository.ts:55-65
WHERE m.room_id = $1
  AND m.sent_at < (
    SELECT sent_at FROM messages WHERE message_id = $2 AND room_id = $1
    -- ↑ Returns NULL when beforeId belongs to a different room
  )
ORDER BY m.sent_at DESC LIMIT $3
\`\`\`

**Failure scenario:** Client passes a \`beforeId\` that exists in the DB but for a different room → subquery yields \`NULL\` → outer condition \`< NULL\` → zero rows returned → client believes it has reached the beginning of message history, silently losing all older messages.

## Tasks

- [ ] Add a \`NULL\` guard: if the subquery returns \`NULL\` (cursor not found in this room), throw a \`NotFoundError\` or \`ValidationError\` instead of returning empty results
- [ ] Add a unit test asserting that a cross-room \`beforeId\` produces an error, not an empty array

## Acceptance criteria

- Passing a \`beforeId\` from a different room returns a 400/404 error, not an empty array
- Valid same-room \`beforeId\` continues to paginate correctly
title: fix: message pagination cursor returns silent empty page when beforeId is from a different room
fix: read_receipt socket event sets lastReadId to arbitrary cross-room message IDs

body:
## Description

In \`friendController.ts\`, the \`respondRequest\` handler's rejection branch calls \`repo.deleteFriendship()\` with no filter on \`status = 'pending'\`. Because \`deleteFriendship\` matches on any row between the two users regardless of status, it can silently delete an already-accepted friendship.

## Evidence

\`\`\`typescript
// backend/src/controllers/friendController.ts:74-79
} else {
  await repo.deleteFriendship(requesterId, userId);  // no status guard
  res.status(200).json({ status: 'rejected' });
}
\`\`\`

\`\`\`sql
-- backend/src/repositories/friendRepository.ts
DELETE FROM friendships
WHERE (requester_id = $1 AND addressee_id = $2)
   OR (requester_id = $2 AND addressee_id = $1)
-- Missing: AND status = 'pending'
\`\`\`

**Failure scenario:** User A and User B are already friends (\`status = 'accepted'\`). A calls \`POST /friends/:id/respond\` with \`{ status: 'rejected' }\` → the accepted friendship row is deleted with no error or warning.

## Tasks

- [ ] Add \`AND status = 'pending'\` to the \`deleteFriendship\` SQL query, or create a separate \`rejectFriendRequest(requesterId, addresseeId)\` repository method that filters on status
- [ ] Return 404 when the pending request to reject is not found
- [ ] Add E2E test asserting that rejecting a request does not delete an accepted friendship

## Acceptance criteria

- Rejecting a friend request only removes \`status = 'pending'\` rows
- Calling the reject endpoint when no pending request exists returns 404
- Accepted friendships are unaffected by the reject endpoint
title: fix: rejecting a friend request silently deletes accepted friendships

body:
## Description

The \`join_room\` WebSocket event handler in \`socketServer.ts\` calls \`socket.join()\` without checking whether the authenticated user is a member of the requested room. Authentication (who you are) is enforced by \`authSocket.ts\`, but authorization (whether you belong to this room) is completely absent.

## Evidence

\`\`\`typescript
// backend/src/realtime/socketServer.ts:36
socket.on('join_room', ({ roomId }) => {
  socket.join(\`room_\${roomId}\`);  // no membership check
});
\`\`\`

Any authenticated user can emit \`join_room\` with an arbitrary \`roomId\` and will immediately start receiving every broadcast to that room channel, including \`new_message\`, \`message_recalled\`, \`user_typing\`, and \`read_update\` events — leaking all private chat content in real time.

## Tasks

- [ ] Before calling \`socket.join()\`, verify that \`userId\` is an active member of \`roomId\` via \`roomMemberRepository.findMember()\`
- [ ] Emit an \`error\` event and return early if membership check fails
- [ ] Add a socket E2E test asserting that a non-member cannot receive room messages

## Acceptance criteria

- A user who is not a member of a room cannot join its socket channel
- Attempting to join a non-member room emits an \`error\` event to the caller
- Existing E2E socket tests continue to pass
title: fix: join_room socket event allows any authenticated user to eavesdrop on any room

body:
## Description
`docs/report-1.md` lists end-to-end encryption as a post-first-stage challenge: store message history as ciphertext and decrypt/search locally on the frontend.

This is not required for the current closed backend issue set, but it is part of the design notes and should be tracked explicitly so teammates know whether it is in or out of scope.

## Tasks
- Decide whether E2E encryption is in scope for the course submission.
- If in scope, define key management, encrypted message payloads, attachment behavior, and local search strategy.
- If out of scope, mark docs accordingly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Team has a clear scope decision.
- Docs no longer leave E2E encryption ambiguous.
- If implemented, backend stores ciphertext only and frontend handles encryption/decryption.
title: enhancement: Track optional end-to-end encryption stretch goal

body:
## Description
Docs describe soft-delete and audit/read-only behavior, but the current schema and services only partially represent it.

Evidence from docs:
- `docs/er_diagram.md` mentions `User.deleted_at`, soft-delete, audit tracking, `is_readonly`, and `needs_audit`.
- `docs/report-1.md` says deleted user messages should remain, marked as deleted sender.
- Docs also say private chats become read-only after block/delete friend.

Current state:
- `users` has no `deleted_at` column.
- `chat_rooms` has `is_archived`, but no explicit `is_readonly` or block/delete-friend room state handling.
- Messages keep `sender_id` with `ON DELETE SET NULL`, but there is no account deletion endpoint/soft-delete flow.

## Tasks
- Decide final schema for soft delete and read-only rooms.
- Add user soft-delete flow if required by docs.
- Ensure historical messages remain readable with a deleted-sender representation.
- Make blocked/deleted-friend private rooms read-only if that remains the intended behavior.
- Add tests for deletion/block history preservation and denied sends.

## Acceptance criteria
- Account deletion does not destroy historical message context.
- Deleted senders render through API in a stable documented shape.
- Read-only rooms reject new sends through REST/Socket flow.
- Docs, schema, and shared types agree.
title: feat: Implement account soft-delete and chat readonly/audit semantics

body:
## Description
Docs describe group ownership and lifecycle rules that are only partially implemented.

Evidence from docs:
- `docs/report-1.md` says every group must have exactly one owner.
- Owners must transfer ownership before leaving.
- Owners can archive/delete groups.
- Admins can manage ordinary members but not other admins/owners.
- UI docs include delete group and member management settings.

Current state:
- Owner leave is blocked, but there is no ownership transfer endpoint.
- `roomService.delete()` exists but is not mounted as a route.
- `isArchived` can be patched, but there is no complete lifecycle contract around archived/read-only rooms.
- Role update can set roles but does not explicitly enforce exactly one owner or provide a safe transfer flow.

## Tasks
- Add ownership transfer endpoint/flow.
- Enforce exactly one owner per group.
- Expose and authorize group delete/archive behavior.
- Define archived group behavior for message sending and member updates.
- Add E2E tests for owner transfer, delete/archive, and owner invariants.

## Acceptance criteria
- An owner can transfer ownership to another member.
- A group cannot have zero owners or multiple owners.
- Owner leave succeeds only after transfer.
- Group delete/archive behavior matches docs and is tested.
title: feat: Complete group lifecycle, ownership transfer, and deletion rules

body:
## Description
Friendship/block endpoints exist, but the response shape and edge cases are not yet product-ready for frontend integration.

Evidence:
- `FriendRepository.getFriends()` returns rows shaped like `{ user_id }`, not `PublicUser` or camelCase contract data.
- Friend request responses use snake_case fields (`requester_id`, `addressee_id`) while most shared/backend contracts use camelCase above repository level.
- The DB primary key allows both A -> B and B -> A pending rows because it only keys `(requester_id, addressee_id)`.
- There is no shared type for the REST friendship responses.

## Tasks
- Define shared response types for friend requests and friend list items.
- Return camelCase fields from controllers/services.
- Decide whether friend list should include `PublicUser` profile data instead of only ids.
- Prevent duplicate reciprocal pending requests or handle them as accept/merge behavior.
- Add E2E tests for reciprocal request behavior and response shapes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Friendship REST responses follow the shared camelCase API contract.
- Frontend can render friend lists without extra guessing.
- A -> B and B -> A cannot create confusing duplicate pending requests.
- Tests cover accepted, rejected, duplicate, reciprocal, and blocked cases.
title: feat: Normalize friendship API responses and reciprocal request behavior

body:
## Description
CI currently verifies backend unit/integration/e2e tests, but it does not run on `dev` pushes and does not validate the frontend.

Evidence:
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs on pushes to `main`, `feat/**`, and `fix/**`, but not `dev`.
- CI has backend unit/integration/e2e jobs.
- CI does not run frontend `tsc --noEmit`, lint, or build.

## Tasks
- Add `dev` to CI push branches.
- Add a frontend job that installs frontend dependencies and runs at least `pnpm --prefix frontend exec tsc --noEmit`.
- Consider adding `pnpm --prefix frontend run lint` and/or `pnpm --prefix frontend run build` if feasible.
- Make sure CI uses the same Node/pnpm versions as local Docker.

## Acceptance criteria
- Pushing to `dev` triggers CI.
- Frontend TypeScript errors fail CI.
- Backend jobs continue to pass.
title: ci: Run checks on dev and add frontend validation

body:
## Description
Several docs/contracts are now stale or inconsistent after the backend feature work.

Examples:
- `shared/types.ts` still says Folder, Attachment, Friendship, Block, EmergencyContact, and MessageMention are deferred, but many are implemented.
- `docs/api-documentation.md` contains routes/events that are partially implemented or missing, such as `GET /rooms/:id/members` and `emergency_alert`.
- Some docs appear to have encoding/garbled text, making them hard for teammates to review.
- `backend/src/AGENTS.md` still describes older inline route structure.

## Tasks
- Update `shared/types.ts` comments to reflect current implemented scope.
- Update `docs/api-documentation.md` to match actual backend routes/events.
- Update `docs/frontend_integration_guide.md` after frontend is wired to real APIs.
- Fix or regenerate garbled docs text where possible.
- Update backend agent docs that still describe old inline handlers.

## Acceptance criteria
- Docs match the actual Express routes and Socket.IO events.
- Deferred/implemented status is accurate.
- Teammates can use docs to implement frontend without guessing.
- No route/event appears in docs unless implemented or explicitly marked planned.
title: chore: Align docs and shared contract with implemented backend scope

body:
## Description
Attachment upload/download APIs exist, and `ClientToServerEvents.send_message` includes `attachments?: string[]`, but the Socket.IO `send_message` handler ignores attachments.

Current state:
- `POST /api/v1/attachments` uploads a file for a `messageId`.
- `GET /api/v1/attachments/:id` downloads it.
- `backend/src/realtime/socketServer.ts` only reads `{ roomId, content, replyTo }` from `send_message`.
- Messages returned by `MessageRepository` do not include attachment metadata.

## Tasks
- Decide attachment flow: upload before message, upload after message, or send message with attachment IDs.
- Persist attachment/message association consistently.
- Include attachment metadata in message responses/broadcasts if UI needs it.
- Validate sender has access to the room/message before attaching files.
- Add E2E tests for sending/displaying a message with attachments.

## Acceptance criteria
- A user can send a message with attachments and other room members receive usable attachment metadata.
- Attachment IDs in `send_message` are validated and linked to the created message.
- Message history includes attachment metadata.
- Backend tests cover the flow.
title: feat: Wire message attachments into send_message flow

body:
## Description
The backend issues are closed and typed wrappers exist in `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` and `frontend/src/lib/socket.ts`, but the actual frontend screens still use mock/local state.

Evidence:
- `frontend/src/app/login/page.tsx` and `frontend/src/app/register/page.tsx` write `mock-jwt-token` to localStorage.
- `frontend/src/context/ChatContext.tsx` stores mock rooms, folders, messages, attachments, read states, and simulated replies.
- The app does not currently call the typed wrappers from `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` / `frontend/src/lib/socket.ts`.

## Tasks
- Wire login/register pages to `login()` / `register()` and store the real JWT/user response.
- Load rooms and messages from backend APIs instead of mock state.
- Connect chat send/recall/typing/read receipt to Socket.IO wrappers.
- Wire folder create/list/update/delete to backend folder APIs.
- Wire attachment upload/download UI to `/api/v1/attachments`.
- Replace simulated read/reply behavior with real socket events.

## Acceptance criteria
- Register -> login -> list rooms -> open room -> list messages works against backend.
- Sending a message uses Socket.IO `send_message` and receives `new_message`.
- Folders and attachments persist after page refresh.
- No `mock-jwt-token` remains in frontend source.
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` passes in frontend container.
title: feat: Connect frontend UI to real backend APIs and Socket.IO
